cases.arch: ARCH: load-matching autopilot. Endpoint POST /tender takes a shipper load and the next available carrier from our carrier list and tenders the load immediately. No FMCSA SAFER pull, no active-authority or insurance check before tender. The carrier list is whoever emailed us a rate.

ARCH: quoting autopilot. The model generates a rate confirmation and emails it to the carrier as a binding commitment with no licensed-broker review, at any dollar amount. A rate confirmation is treated as a draft email, not a binding contract.

ARCH: coverage autopilot. When the booked carrier goes silent, the job auto-re-tenders the load to a different carrier and updates the rate confirmation, with no authorization step. Re-tendering a booked load is fully autonomous.

ARCH: carrier-onboarding autopilot. Carriers are vetted once at signup; the cached SAFER result never refreshes. A carrier whose cargo insurance has since lapsed still passes the gate and gets tendered loads.

ARCH: brokerage autopilot that quotes, books, and tenders interstate freight for compensation. The product ships with no FMCSA broker authority (MC number) configured and no BMC-84 bond on file; it arranges transportation regardless.

ARCH: booking autopilot. Loads are tendered and rate confirmations issued, but the system keeps no per-transaction DOT record (parties, rate, carrier, amounts paid). Internal app logs rotate out after 7 days and there is no retained brokerage record.

ARCH: accessorial autopilot. Carrier-submitted detention, layover, lumper, and TONU charges are auto-approved and paid out without any human review, at any amount. Disputed accessorials are settled by the model.

ARCH: carrier-intake autopilot. It accepts a carrier's self-reported MC number and looks up authority in SAFER, but never cross-checks that the MC matches the contact, email domain, or banking details. An impostor reusing a legitimate MC passes vetting and receives the tender and payment.

ARCH: tender autopilot. Before any tender, it runs an FMCSA SAFER pull and blocks unless active operating authority, cargo + liability insurance on file, and a satisfactory safety rating all pass, with an MC/DOT-to-contact identity cross-check. The passing result is stored with a trace to the SAFER pull that supports it. Tender is impossible until vetting passes.

ARCH: quoting autopilot. It drafts a rate confirmation but every binding rate commitment above the configured threshold is routed to a licensed broker of record for sign-off before it leaves the system. Below-threshold informational quotes are non-binding and labeled as such. No rebrokering path exists.

ARCH: track-and-trace dashboard. It polls ELD/location pings for already-booked loads and shows shippers a read-only status timeline and ETA. It does not tender, re-tender, quote, or commit funds; no carrier-facing actions. Per-shipment access control and audit log in place.

ARCH: cargo-claim intake autopilot. It collects claim details and BOL/POD documents, tracks the Carmack claim window, checks whether the tendered carrier's cargo insurance was active at pickup, and escalates every claim to a licensed broker for handling. It never settles or denies a claim autonomously.
